meta.feed_title: Latest World News on Fox News

items.title: Iran secures UN role with backing from UK, France, Canada, Australia as US stands alone
items.summary: Western democracies face backlash for allowing Iran and other authoritarian regimes to secure influential U.N. seats as the U.S. stood alone in opposition.

items.title: Pakistani general says Iran diplomacy still alive, despite US blockade, failed talks
items.summary: The U.S. naval blockade on Iran may be leverage to resume diplomacy, says retired Pakistani general, as both sides were reportedly close to a deal.

items.title: Could Somaliland base emerge as US foothold against Iran, Houthis in key sea lanes?
items.summary: Somaliland is offering the U.S. a Red Sea port and air base at Berbera as Iran pushes Houthis to disrupt shipping through the Bab-el-Mandeb strait.

items.title: China says it will resume some ties with Taiwan after visit by opposition leader
items.summary: China says it will restore some ties with Taiwan, including direct flights and aquaculture imports, following a visit by opposition leader Cheng Li-wun.

items.title: ‘Gate of Tears’ at risk: Iran threatens major new global chokepoint if US moves on Hormuz
items.summary: Senior Middle East advisor warns Iran could use Houthi proxies to blockade the Bab al-Mandeb strait if the U.S. blocks the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Iran in crisis as US talks collapse, Mojtaba’s ‘mafia’ regime blocks Khamenei burial: analyst
items.summary: Analyst says Iran's delay in burying Supreme Leader Khamenei over 40 days after his killing reveals a deepening crisis inside the Islamic Republic.

items.title: Pope Leo XIV to visit fastest-growing Catholic continent during 4-nation Africa trip
items.summary: Pope Leo XIV begins a four-nation African visit Monday, traveling to the fastest-growing continent for the Catholic Church on his fourth foreign trip.

items.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat as opposition leader heads for potential supermajority win
items.summary: Orbán conceded defeat Sunday as opposition leader Péter Magyar surged toward a landslide victory, signaling a major political shift in Hungary.

items.title: Hungarians vote in record numbers as Trump-ally Viktor Orbán faces biggest electoral threat since 2010
items.summary: Hungarians vote in pivotal election pitting PM Viktor Orbán against rival Peter Magyar. Vice President JD Vance visited Hungary last week and met with the prime minister.

items.title: IDF uncovers Hezbollah weapons stash inside hospital in Lebanon
items.summary: Israel Defense Forces uncovered a weapons stash used by Hezbollah terrorists inside a hospital in Lebanon this weekend.

items.title: Ukraine, Russia claim thousands of violations of Putin-imposed holiday ceasefire
items.summary: Russian President Vladimir Putin's 32-hour Orthodox Easter ceasefire appeared to unravel as Russia and Ukraine traded accusations of thousands of violations, deepening mutual mistrust.

items.title: Pope Leo calls out 'delusion of omnipotence' fueling Iran war in vigil for peace at St. Peter's Basilica
items.summary: Pope Leo decries the "delusion of omnipotence" he says is driving the war in Iran, urging peace during a Saturday vigil at St. Peter's Basilica.

items.title: Malnourished boy found locked in father's van for more than a year unable to walk, hadn't showered since 2024
items.summary: A malnourished 9-year-old boy allegedly locked in his father's van in eastern France was found in the fetal position surrounded by trash and excrement.

items.title: Man climbs onto US military aircraft in Ireland, attacks it with hatchet: report
items.summary: A man allegedly climbed onto a U.S. military aircraft at Ireland's Shannon Airport and attacked it with a hatchet, according to local police reports.

items.title: Iran's supreme leader severely disfigured by US strikes: report
items.summary: Iranian Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei is severely disfigured after U.S.-Israel airstrikes on Tehran killed his father, according to a Reuters report citing anonymous sources.

items.title: Iran regime uses former Soviet republic to dodge sanctions, fund war machine: report
items.summary: Once a committed U.S. ally, Georgia has quietly aligned with Iran, raising concerns about IRGC influence and sanctions evasion, a new report claims.

items.title: Taiwan opposition leader meets Xi in Beijing as Taiwan defense fight intensifies
items.summary: Xi Jinping met KMT leader Cheng Li-wun in Beijing as Taiwan's opposition blocks a $40 billion defense budget amid U.S. pressure to approve it.

items.title: Iran’s ‘Godfather of propaganda’ tactics resurface in war with US and Israel
items.summary: Experts say Iran uses civilians and children as human shields for propaganda purposes, caring nothing for the safety and fate of its own people.

items.title: Inside Tehran after strikes: Iranian woman describes fear, checkpoints and people used as ‘human shields’
items.summary: An anonymous Iranian woman detailed to The Australian the nightly explosions, celebrations and fear in Tehran as Trump's two-week ceasefire with Iran tentatively took hold.

items.title: Israeli man built bomb lab for Iranian plot targeting ex-PM Bennett, authorities say
items.summary: A Haifa man was allegedly recruited by an Iranian handler to build bombs for an assassination plot believed to target ex-PM Naftali Bennett.

items.title: UK defense minister warns Putin of 'serious consequences' after covert underwater military operation
items.summary: U.K. Defense Minister John Healey has revealed a major military operation involving the Royal Navy and Norway to deter Russian submarines in the North Sea.

items.title: Strait of Hormuz toll would set 'dangerous precedent,' UN shipping agency warns
items.summary: The U.N.'s shipping agency warned on Thursday that any toll imposed on ships passing through the Strait of Hormuz would set a "dangerous precedent."

items.title: Miner rescued alive after spending nearly 2 weeks trapped underground in flooded area
items.summary: A Mexican miner was rescued after spending 13 days trapped underground, officials said, while one miner was found dead and another remains missing.

items.title: US allowing some Nigeria embassy staff to evacuate over 'deteriorating security situation'
items.summary: State Department authorized departure of some U.S. embassy staff in Nigeria over a "deteriorating security situation" linked to terrorism and violence.

items.title: Chief prosecutor behind Israel war crimes charges faces disciplinary action amid sexual misconduct allegations
items.summary: ICC Chief Prosecutor Karim Khan faces disciplinary proceedings over alleged sexual misconduct after the Bureau of the Assembly of States Parties voted to move forward with action.
